features.name: Serverless Data Warehouse
features.description: Executes SQL queries across petabytes of structured data without managing servers, indexes, or clusters.

features.name: Separated Storage & Compute
features.description: Decouples query processing slots from physical data storage, optimizing performance and budget allocations.

features.name: Real-Time Machine Learning
features.description: Enables training and running machine learning models directly inside the data warehouse using standard SQL.

iamRoles.description: Full access to BigQuery Data Transfer resources, datasets, tables, queries, and security settings.
Read-only access to datasets, tables, views, and execution results.

cliExamples.description: Load a CSV file from Cloud Storage into a BigQuery table
Run a SQL query and save output to a destination table
cliExamples.command:
bq query --destination_table=my-dataset.results-table \
  --use_legacy_sql=false \
  'SELECT user_id, SUM(amount) FROM `my-project.my-dataset.sales` GROUP BY user_id'

design: BigQuery is a serverless, highly scalable cloud data warehouse for structured and semi-structured data.
Decoupled architecture scales storage and compute slots independently based on active queries.
Partitioning tables (by ingestion time, date/timestamp, or integer range) limits data scanned during queries.
Clustering tables orders data based on column values, working with partitioning to speed up query execution.
BigQuery Omni allows running queries across multi-cloud environments (AWS S3, Azure Blob Storage).
Dataproc runs managed Hadoop and Spark clusters, migrating legacy on-premises big data systems to GCP.
BigQuery BI Engine caches query states in memory to accelerate dashboard and report rendering.
Use materialized views to cache the results of complex queries and speed up downstream analytics.
security: Authorize views to share query results with users without giving them access to the underlying tables.
Enforce row-level security and column-level security using policy tags to restrict access to sensitive fields.
Manage BigQuery access using IAM dataset roles (`roles/bigquery.dataViewer`, `roles/bigquery.admin`).
Encrypt analytical datasets at rest using Customer-Managed Encryption Keys (CMEK) via Cloud KMS.
VPC Service Controls perimeters protect analytical databases from data exfiltration and public queries.
Audit data-access logs to monitor which service accounts and users are running queries on datasets.
Mask sensitive PII fields inside table columns automatically using Sensitive Data Protection integrations.
Secure connection access by disabling public datasets whitelisting on internal project datasets.
reliability: BigQuery replicates datasets across multiple physical data centers in the selected region.
Datasets can be configured as multi-regional (e.g. US or EU) for geographical redundancy and failover.
Time travel enables recovering deleted or modified table states up to 7 days in the past.
Dataproc clusters can be configured as high availability, deploying three master nodes to prevent control plane failures.
Write queries using standard SQL with built-in query optimization and syntax verification.
System metrics, query execution logs, and load jobs are logged automatically to Cloud Logging.
Failed query jobs due to transient errors support automated client retries with backoff.
Export datasets to Cloud Storage regularly to create offline historical database backups.
cost: BigQuery storage is billed per GB/month; active storage transitions to long-term storage (50% discount) if unmodified for 90 days.
Query billing has two models: On-demand (billed per TB of data scanned) and Capacity (flat-rate billing using slots).
Prune scanned data and reduce costs by always including partitioning columns in query WHERE clauses.
Avoid using SELECT *; always query only the specific columns needed to minimize TB scan size.
Use slots reservations to allocate query budgets across development, staging, and production teams.
Set maximum query billing limits in the console to block run-away queries from running.
Clean up temporary tables and query outputs by configuring table expiration policies.
Review cost optimization recommendation logs to identify infrequently queried tables to archive.
